threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' when you mean the train from Hackney to Shoreditch. Locals know it’s just the London Transport lifeline.
Last night on the 59, a couple were arguing about which one of them forgot to buy the milk. Their voices echoed all down the aisle.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week, I saw a guy feeding pigeons while his toddler practiced German with them. It's those little things that make you realize you're truly home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a street art festival running parallel to a beer garden's live DJ set, with both spilling into the same sidewalk.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their disdain for tourists blocking the sidewalk outside Tartine at peak brunch hours. We know better.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo juggling on the bus while a lady ranted about the BART delays—only in San Francisco, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real heart is in the local festivals—nobody knows about the correfoc until you witness it.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can usually tell by the way they hesitate at crosswalks, waiting for a green light while locals smoothly cross whenever.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's acceptable to ask for directions to the nearest tube station. Everyone knows the buses are the real lifeblood around here.
Last night on the 77, a guy argued with a lamp post for 10 minutes. Didn't even notice when the bus left him behind.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Walking past the Turkish bakery and hearing an old man explain the perfect way to roll simit dough to his grandkids.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a spontaneous dance party at a U-Bahn station when a train is delayed. The whole platform turns into a club!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local when they can name at least three taco trucks that still open after midnight—it's a survival skill here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: I saw a guy in a full tuxedo playing the accordion on the Muni to the Symphony. Only in San Francisco do you get pre-gaming like that.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí. They miss the tiny art galleries tucked between bakeries, showcasing local talent and heart.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their hesitation at the crosswalks, waiting for the green light even when it’s safe to go. They don’t yet trust the rhythm of the city.
